Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The Industrial Revolution was in full swing and women ventured beyond their homes into the workplace and politics. Angela Wolf creates a luxurious kimono duster inspired by those worn over long, full skirts and dresses. Kathy McMakin uses today's machines to create a precious heirloom baby bonnet you'll treasure.
Video.title: The New Century Begins - The 1900's

Video.summary: As quilters, we make lots of HSTs (half-square triangles). If you like the two-at-a-time method, Angela has the hack for you! Watch as she uses painter's tape to sew quarter-inch seams instead of marking each square.
Video.grandparentTitle: Bonus: Painter's Tape for Faster Quilting
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Author and designer Kristal Wick has some fabulous stringing projects featuring double drilled beads. She shows how to design coordinating necklace and bracelet using double drilled beads. Kristal also has a segment discussing metal clay. Katie shows how to store and organize seed beads.
Video.title: Stringing Projects Using Double-drilled Beads

Video.summary: In the episode the craftsman guides viewers through essential techniques and considerations for crafting cabinet doors with precision and efficiency. He begins by stressing the importance of wood selection, advising against woods with prominent grain patterns like oak, which are unsuitable for door frames. Instead, he recommends using straight-grained pieces from the edges of the wood or opting for materials like MDF, birch, or maple for paint-grade projects due to their smooth surfaces and availability in large sheets. The craftsman demonstrates how to prepare materials for construction, including ripping pieces for frames and face frames, and discusses the suitability of poplar for practice due to its affordability and ease of machining. When it comes to door panels, he explains the careful use of plywood and the importance of adjusting grooves precisely for optimal fit. He showcases the use of a dado head to create tenons that snugly fit into these grooves, ensuring strong and gap-free assembly. Techniques for creating specific door styles are highlighted, such as using half-lap joints adjusted on the table saw for flush fits. The craftsman emphasizes marking and consistent cuts to avoid errors, crucial for achieving perfect joints in door frame assembly. He demonstrates assembly steps and discusses adding decorative pegs for both aesthetic appeal and structural integrity, particularly useful for heavier doors like those with leaded glass panels. Throughout the episode, the craftsman underscores the use of templates for ensuring consistent shapes, particularly useful for cathedral-style doors. He employs a band saw and router table with double-sided tape to achieve precision in shaping components, emphasizing careful setup and sequential cuts for optimal woodworking results. Overall, the episode provides comprehensive guidance on wood selection, tool adjustment, joint creation, and assembly techniques, empowering woodworkers to create high-quality cabinet doors effectively.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Crafting Cabinet Doors: Materials & Cutting Techniques

Image.alt: Crafting Cabinet Doors: Materials & Cutting Techniques
Crafting Cabinet Doors: Materials & Cutting Techniques
Crafting Cabinet Doors: Materials & Cutting Techniques

Video.summary: Everything old is new in the hands of the modern quilter. Pattern designer Elizabeth Dackson reinterprets vintage blocks using a modern aesthetic. Brenda Ratliff and Heather Grant trade tips on booking the faculty for quilt events. Modern quilt enthusiast Elizabeth Dackson uses half-square triangles to create dynamic blocks.
Video.title: Old and New

Video.summary: CAUTION: Baby quilts are a total gateway drug! Just ask Angela: she's working this cute little number up for her newborn nephew. (Think you can stop at one? Don't say we didn't warn you!)
Video.title: Z Is for Zoo

Video.summary: Angela's reminded of her age as she relies on her kids to keep up with the digital world. Watch as she uses two basic shapes to create a mod half-square triangle quilt that's easier to make than it looks.
Video.title: Modern Technology Half-Square Triangle Quilt

Video.summary: Season 10 of "The Midnight Quilt Show" wraps with Angela making modern ombré wall hanging to display in a *very* exclusive art exhibition.
Video.title: Modern Ombré Wall Hanging

Video.summary: Angela's got stars in her eyes this week as she pieces together the eight-pointed star Twinkling Twilight quilt by Nancy Smith.
Video.title: Twinkling Twilight

Video.summary: This is not a drill! Angela has heard your requests loud and clear, and she's finally making the Bargello quilt that everyone's been asking for! So break out the layer cakes and prepare to get crazy with precuts.
Video.title: Modern Bargello

Video.summary: Can’t get enough of bargello? Angela Walters shows you how to make a ravishing rainbow quilt using precut fabric strip sets.
Video.title: Bargello Beauty Rainbow Quilt

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: Are you Quilt Curious, but unsure where to get started? THIS is the Midnight Quilt Show episode for you!
Video.title: Friendship Stars Quilt

Video.summary: In the Season 5 finale of The Midnight Quilt show, Angela is creating a modern quilt of her own design that involves a 36-patch block!
Video.title: Modern X

Video.summary: Believe it or not, this quilt is only made of straight seams! Watch it "unfold" as Angela conquers the Strip City pattern with Boundless blues.
Video.title: Strip City

Video.summary: We were headed to the moon! Back on earth, the hippie movement took hold with loose-fitting, fluid fashions, bell bottoms, sandals, t-shirts and headbands. Dressing up included a ruffled shirt, striped hip-hugger pants and zippered boots. Londa Rohlfing shares her sewing expertise to add ruffles to any garment and Coleen Swettman sews a hippie-inspired poncho that goes everywhere today.
Video.title: The Counterculture - The Late 1960's

Video.summary: Time can be an element of jewelry design and this episode is all about speed – quick and easy projects that include prefinished components and unique materials. First, Leslie Rogalski shows how to stitch a suite of variations of finished jewelry using shaped beads and metal fashion components; mix and match for quick designing. Then, Julie Fei Fan Balzer, the host of Make It Artsy, stops by with cork earrings.
Video.title: Quick and Easy

Video.summary: This season is all about learning from the masters but we'd be remiss if we didn't also take time to feature some modern ingenuity. Katie takes a look at beads inspired by Monet as a nod to the past. Then Aja Vaz brings us into the current world with modern torches and making tube shaped beads. Then Katie is back with old meets new, as we look at new ways to set crystals. Finally Tammy shows us new and old loom types.
Video.title: Modern Ingenuity

Video.summary: Polish it and make it shine. Amy Tan makes pages gleam with foil and shares her favorite tape storage tip. Christine Drumheller adds bling and banners to her memory pages. Joe Rotella creates a shimmering lantern that lights up the nighttime.
Video.title: Episode 1: Shine It Up

Video.summary: It's time to celebrate. May Flaum starts the party with a special birthday party invitations and a shout-out page. Joe Rotella makes charming thank-you gift sets for hostesses. May Flaum has a tip for great backgrounds. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer celebrates Girls' Night Out with a page made with stamp rollers and sticker tape.
Video.title: Episode 7: Celebrations

Video.summary: There is no better place to find inspiration than outdoors -- a walk outside can change your whole outlook. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer opens the show creating leaf luminaires inspired by the outside and great to use outdoors. Then, Erin Gerlach creates a bee or butterfly basin to attract these creatures to your garden. The show closes with a color study -- the color green.
Video.title: Episode 2: Your Outdoor Space

Video.summary: Weather affects our mood, and the only thing constant about it is change! Not only does it change by season but day to day and sometimes multiple times in one day. First, host Julie Fei Fan Balzer celebrates the weather with a bullet journal with a place to remember the weather. Then create dimensional snowflakes with Gail Wild. Finally, Lynn Lilly is back with a weather family photo board to let everyone know just what to expect each day!
Video.title: Episode 11: Weather

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Cozy is calling! Join the ladies in an ode to warm projects, winter stews and all things bulky.
Video.title: Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects

Image.alt: Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects
Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects
Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects

Video.summary: The Grocery Girls will show you how to join the sides of your Novel-T. Jodi demonstrates how to pick up and knit the provisional stitches then shows you how to start the lace panel pattern.
Video.title: Picking Up Provisional Stitches & Cable Cast-On for Lace Panels

Image.alt: Picking Up Provisional Stitches & Cable Cast-On for Lace Panels
Picking Up Provisional Stitches & Cable Cast-On for Lace Panels
Picking Up Provisional Stitches & Cable Cast-On for Lace Panels

Video.summary: When creative ideas pop up, write them down. Make a colorful pocket journal with Jenny Barnett-Rohrs. Candie Cooper shares creative tips for handwriting and Franz Spohn writes with clay. Katie Hacker decorates for fall with a shining pumpkin luminary from blogger Carissa Bonham.
Video.title: Write It Down

Video.summary: Bring the outside inside with a little creativity. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes a colorful leafy tree banner. Candie Cooper creates a fish kite and Katie Hacker builds a whimsical woods scene. Then, Jenny decorates a glittery tree to celebrate the holidays.
Video.title: Trees

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
